title: Docs: add observability hooks reference for quickstart templates
preview:
Title: Docs: add observability hooks reference for quickstart templates

Context
We’re shipping production-minded RAG + agent quickstart templates that emit standardized OpenTelemetry signals plus Redwood-specific conventions. Support/SRE feedback has been consistent: we need a canonical reference page (1) defining the required spans/attributes/metrics/log fields, and (2) mapping those to Redwood Console dashboards so users can verify instrumentation quickly and understand what they’re looking at.

What this PR does
- Adds a new docs reference page: "Observability hooks (reference)" for Quickstart Templates.
- Documents required spans + span attributes (including what is REQUIRED vs RECOMMENDED).
- Documents required metrics (names, types, units, and allowed dimensions) and includes a “cardinality guardrails” section aligned with Observability/SRE guidance.
- Adds a “Mapping to Redwood Console” section that calls out which dashboards/panels use which signals (Templates Overview, RAG Latency Breakdown, Tool Failures, Retrieval Quality, Token Economics).
- Adds a small “How to validate locally” checklist (view trace tree, confirm metrics are flowing, confirm request-id correlation).

Non-goals
- This does not introduce new instrumentation in templates/SDKs; it’s documentation only.
- This does not document every possible OTel semantic convention; we focus on the minimum set needed to make templates supportable and dashboards functional.

Docs IA / links
- Linked from the Quickstart Templates landing page and the RAG+Agent walkthrough.
- Cross-linked from the Troubleshooting page ("No traces in Console?" and "Missing tool spans?").

New/updated content (high level)
1) Tracing
- Required span hierarchy (expected parent/child relationships):
  - http.server (or equivalent framework span)
    - redwood.llm.request
      - redwood.rag.retrieve (optional for non-RAG)
      - redwood.agent.iteration (0..N)
        - redwood.tool.<tool_name> (0..N)
- Required span attributes (REQUIRED):
  - redwood.request_id (from API response header when available; used for support correlation)
  - llm.system = "redwood" (or llm.provider = "redwood"; chosen term documented for consistency)
  - llm.model (pinned model identifier)
  - llm.operation ("chat" | "responses" | "embeddings" | "rerank")
  - http.response.status_code (for the client call span)
  - error.type (normalized error taxonomy keys)
- Recommended attributes (RECOMMENDED) with cardinality cautions:
  - redwood.deployment_mode ("hosted" | "dedicated" | "private")
  - redwood.region
  - redwood.template.name / redwood.template.version (for template-originated traffic tagging)
  - tool.name (low-cardinality) and tool.result (DO NOT log raw output)
- Redaction rules:
  - Explicitly prohibit recording prompt/user content as span attributes.
  - Recommend hashing document IDs if they must be included.
  - Provide examples of safe logging fields.

2) Metrics
- Required metrics (names, types, and units):
  - redwood.llm.latency_ms (histogram)
  - redwood.llm.tokens.input (counter)
  - redwood.llm.tokens.output (counter)
  - redwood.llm.requests (counter)
  - redwood.llm.errors (counter)
  - redwood.rag.retrieval.latency_ms (histogram)
  - redwood.rag.retrieval.results_count (histogram)
  - redwood.tool.calls (counter)
  - redwood.tool.errors (counter)
- Required metric dimensions (allowed list):
  - llm.model, llm.operation, redwood.template.name, redwood.template.version
- Forbidden/high-risk dimensions (explicit):
  - user_id, session_id, request_id, full URL paths, document titles, arbitrary tool args

3) Logs
- Minimal structured log fields to align with support workflows:
  - level, message, timestamp
  - redwood.request_id
  - error.type, error.message (redacted)
  - llm.model, llm.operation
  - tool.name (if relevant)

4) Mapping to Redwood Console
- Dashboard: Templates Overview
  - Uses redwood.template.name/version + redwood.llm.requests + redwood.llm.latency_ms
- Dashboard: Token Economics
  - Uses redwood.llm.tokens.input/output + latency histograms for cost/latency analysis
- Dashboard: Tool Failures
  - Uses redwood.tool.errors + tool spans with error.type/tool.name
- Dashboard: RAG Latency Breakdown
  - Uses redwood.rag.retrieval.latency_ms span timing + llm request span timing

Why this matters
- Reduces "is it instrumented correctly?" support load.
- Makes template dashboards immediately actionable.
- Prevents accidental high-cardinality tags and prompt/PII leakage.

Implementation notes
- Content is aligned with internal standards discussed with Allison/Logan (span naming + attribute set) and SRE feedback (cardinality guardrails, redaction language).
- Added a small admonition block where we previously saw proxy issues with opt-in telemetry headers; the reference clarifies that template tagging is optional and must not include user data.

Screenshots
- N/A (docs-only). Included example trace tree and metric table inline.

Testing
- `pnpm lint`
- `pnpm test:links`
- `pnpm build`

Follow-ups
- After merge, we should link this page from the SDK instrumentation guide (Wei is tracking separately) and ensure the observability pack dashboard names match the final docs strings.


Commits
1) docs(quickstarts): add observability hooks reference page
2) docs(quickstarts): link reference from templates landing + walkthrough
3) docs(observability): add metric tables + cardinality guidance
4) chore(ci): fix link checker failures for new anchors

- docs/quickstarts/templates/_index.md (add reference link)
- docs/quickstarts/templates/rag-agent-walkthrough.md (add “Verify observability” section + link)
- docs/quickstarts/templates/troubleshooting.md (add cross-links to reference)
- docs/partials/otel-attribute-table.md (new reusable table partial)
- docs/partials/otel-metric-table.md (new reusable table partial)
- docs/nav.yml (add page to Quickstart Templates section)

Illustrative snippet (from observability-hooks-reference.md)
Span names (required): http.server -> redwood.llm.request -> (optional) redwood.rag.retrieve -> (optional) redwood.agent.iteration -> (optional) redwood.tool.<tool_name>. Required attributes on redwood.llm.request: llm.model, llm.operation, redwood.request_id (when available), llm.system="redwood".

Review conversation (highlights)
- Allison Grant: "Please call out allowed metric dimensions explicitly and add a warning about request_id/user_id as labels. Also clarify span attribute redaction (no prompts)." Author (Elliot): "Added ‘Allowed dimensions’ list, ‘Forbidden dimensions’ list, and a dedicated redaction section with examples. Updated wording to match quickstart tracing conventions." (resolved)
- Logan Wright: "Mapping to Console: can we be precise about which panels use template tags vs generic OTel? Also note that template tags are opt-in." Author: "Updated mapping section to indicate dashboard filters use `redwood.template.*` tags when present, otherwise dashboards fall back to model/operation aggregates. Added opt-in note + privacy language." (resolved)
- Christine Wong: "Local validation checklist is good. Add a note that missing traces is often collector endpoint/auth, and link to troubleshooting." Author: "Added explicit link to troubleshooting and a short callout for collector auth/endpoints." (resolved)
- Monica Patel: "Please ensure attribute naming matches what templates actually emit (redwood.template.version vs template_version)." Author: "Aligned to template naming convention: `redwood.template.name` and `redwood.template.version`." (resolved)
- Sergio Costa: "Metric names should be consistent with dashboard pack. I think retrieval metric is `redwood.rag.retrieval.latency_ms` not `redwood.rag.retrieve.latency_ms`." Author: "Updated metric names and added a short ‘naming alignment’ note." (resolved)

source.title: Docs: Data residency region pinning (Hosted) — configuration, limitations, and troubleshooting
source.author: Wei Chen
source.description: This PR adds public documentation for Hosted data residency enforcement via region pinning. It covers: (1) what region pinning guarantees, (2) how to configure allowed regions + primary region in the Console and via Admin API (where available), (3) enforcement behavior (routing + fallback constraints) and common failure modes, (4) the public error contract for residency violations (HTTP status, error_code, request_id, help_url), and (5) troubleshooting steps and support-ready guidance. The content is aligned with the current gateway contract (409 on deterministic policy block) and clarifies limitations/non-goals (no automatic multi-region replication; some non-content account metadata may be processed outside the pinned region). It also includes copy for streaming vs non-streaming behavior and points users to audit log + blocked-request views in Console. Internal-only implementation details are intentionally omitted; this is customer-facing guidance only. Docs note: terminology standardized to “Allowed regions” and “Primary region” per Product guidance; avoids “geo-fencing” language.
commits: docs(security): add hosted data residency overview + guarantees
docs(hosted-api): add region pinning configuration guide (Console + API)
docs(errors): document residency violation error contract (409 + error_code) with examples
docs(troubleshooting): add blocked requests playbook + FAQ links
chore(nav): add data residency pages to Security & Compliance sidebar
notable_snippets: Example: residency policy violation (non-streaming) => HTTP/1.1 409 Conflict; body: {"error":{"type":"policy_error","error_code":"RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION","message":"Request blocked: project is pinned to EU regions (primary: eu-west). This request would be processed in us-east due to routing constraints.","request_id":"req_01HTR8J2Y8Q3K3F2K0W9M2Q6B7","help_url":"https://docs.redwoodinference.com/security/data-residency/troubleshooting#residency-policy-violation","details":{"allowed_regions":"eu-west, eu-central","selected_region":"us-east","policy_scope":"project","policy_id":"rpolj_8fQx..."}}}

Example: streaming behavior => if blocked before tokens are emitted, server returns 409 with the same JSON error payload; if tokens already started, the stream is terminated with an error frame containing error_code=RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION and request_id (SDKs surface this as a dedicated exception).

Configuration summary => Allowed regions controls where customer content may be processed; Primary region is the preferred processing region within the allowed set. Cross-region fallback is disabled by default when pinning is enabled.
review_conversation: Elliot Price (Docs): Please move the strongest guarantees to the top of the overview page and add a single-sentence non-goal disclaimer (no automatic replication) before the config steps. Also make sure we don’t imply “data never leaves the region” without qualifying non-content metadata.

Wei Chen (Author): Updated overview to lead with guarantees, added a “What this does NOT do” callout, and clarified “customer content” vs “account metadata” language. Also added a link to the limitations page from the first section.

Naomi Feldman (Product): Terminology: prefer “Allowed regions” + “Primary region”; avoid “pinned to EU-only” in headings (use “pinned to EU regions”). In troubleshooting, add an explicit “how to fix” checklist (wrong endpoint region, org/project mismatch, key override).
Wei Chen (Author): Applied terminology changes across pages and updated headings. Added troubleshooting checklist + example scenarios (org pinned to EU, project not pinned; key-level override).

Brian Murphy (API): Error contract: the code should match gateway exactly. Use HTTP 409 (not 403) and error_code RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION. Include request_id and help_url; policy_id exposure is OK but mark it as “may be present.” Also make sure examples show details.selected_region and details.allowed_regions.

Wei Chen (Author): Updated errors page + JSON examples to 409 + RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION and added fields per gateway. Marked policy_id as optional. Added note that selected_region may be omitted when the request is blocked prior to region selection.

Marcus Lin (Security Eng): Please add a short section clarifying logging/tracing: we keep customer content in-region; global telemetry may include non-content identifiers (request_id, error_code) outside region. Also remove any mention of internal service names (e.g., “smart-routing”).
Wei Chen (Author): Added “Logging, metrics, and audit logs” section with the content/non-content split; removed internal service names and replaced with “routing layer”/“gateway.”

Kira Thompson (Enterprise Docs): Add a note about Dedicated vs Hosted differences (Dedicated is capacity-isolated but residency behavior aligns when configured; for Private deployments, refer to Private docs). Also add a short FAQ entry: “Can I allow multiple regions?”
Wei Chen (Author): Added a “Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private” section + multi-region allowance FAQ entry (allowed_regions supports multiple; primary_region used for preference).
Elliot Price (Docs): Final nit: anchor links for troubleshooting sections should match sidebar labels; also check that the help_url in the error example uses the public docs domain.
Wei Chen (Author): Fixed anchors, sidebar labels, and updated help_url to https://docs.redwoodinference.com/…
ci_checks: link-checker: pass (rerun once after fixing anchors)
source.merge_outcome: Merged via squash into main. Post-merge: published with next docs deploy (auto).
release_notes: New public docs: Hosted data residency region pinning (Allowed regions + Primary region), limitations, and troubleshooting.
Documented residency policy violation error response with examples (HTTP 409 + RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION).

source.title: Internal docs: add Unit Economics dashboard hub + link runbooks/definitions
source.author: Allison Grant
source.description: This PR updates the internal docs site to include a single “Unit Economics Dashboard” hub page and fixes/standardizes links to the canonical Confluence sources for definitions, SLOs, and oncall procedures. The goal is to reduce ping-pong across Slack threads and PR descriptions by having one stable entry point that Finance/Product/Eng can bookmark. The hub also documents access control expectations (feature flag + RBAC) and explicitly calls out v1 limitations so the metric isn’t over-interpreted. Changes include: (1) new internal docs page that links to the PRD, UX spec, metric dictionary, ADR for cost attribution, and Finance methodology note; (2) links to the SLO page and the oncall runbook (investigation + backfill) plus a short “how to validate freshness/completeness” checklist; (3) updated navigation entry under Observability → Cost & Usage; (4) replaced stale/notionally-correct but broken URLs referenced from older dashboard docs; (5) added a short section on data quality/confidence indicators and what to do when the dashboard disagrees with billing (points to reconciliation report/runbook). No product behavior changes; docs-only.
commits: docs(internal): add unit economics dashboard hub page
docs: update observability nav to include Unit Economics
docs: fix broken confluence links + add reconciliation pointers
docs(security): document RBAC/feature-flag access pattern
chore: run markdown lint + link check fixes
source.conversation: Sean Gallagher (review): Can we make sure the runbook link is the *oncall guide* page, not the older “pipeline notes” doc? Also add the alert routing channel so folks don’t page the wrong team. Allison Grant (author): Updated to point at the oncall guide and SLO page; added a short “Who to page” section with eng-oncall vs eng-sre routing and a note that Finance signoff questions go to #finance. Logan Wright (review): Please call out the streaming token parity caveat explicitly. We had a bunch of confusion last week when tokens were under-counted and people assumed costs jumped. Allison Grant (author): Added “Known limitations / caveats (v1)” section with streaming parity + a pointer to the reconciliation report and the data quality checks page. Marissa Cole (review): The hub should link to the PRD and UX spec; Product wants a stable link for dogfood announcements. Also: can we add a ‘What is blended cost vs marginal cost?’ blurb to prevent misreads? Allison Grant (author): Added PRD + UX spec links; added 5-line definition/interpretation note and a warning that blended includes idle/overhead per ADR. Marcus Lin (security review): Please avoid including direct Grafana links that leak internal org IDs in the URL. Use the shortlink / internal redirect pattern. Allison Grant (author): Replaced raw Grafana URLs with internal shortlinks; also added a note that the dashboard is internal-only and governed by RBAC + feature flag (PM-487). Chloe Martin (review): Could we include the API endpoint docs link for engineers? Not required for Finance, but it helps for troubleshooting. Allison Grant (author): Added “Engineering references” section with API contract sketch link and pointers to the aggregation service doc; kept it in a collapsed details block so the page stays readable. Final: Sean approved after link check passed; merged via squash.

title: Internal docs: add Unit Economics dashboard hub + link runbooks/definitions
preview: This PR updates the internal docs site to include a single “Unit Economics Dashboard” hub page and fixes/standardizes links to the canonical Confluence sources for definitions, SLOs, and oncall procedures. The goal is to reduce ping-pong across Slack threads and PR descriptions by having one stable entry point that Finance/Product/Eng can bookmark. No product behavior changes; docs-only.

source.title: Docs: RBAC v2 concepts, permission catalog, role templates, and migration notes
source.author: Elliot Price
source.description:
Summary
Adds first-pass public documentation for RBAC v2 across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments. This PR introduces: (1) a conceptual overview of RBAC v2 primitives (permissions, roles, bindings, scopes), (2) a permission catalog reference page (with naming conventions and deprecation/versioning notes), (3) built-in role templates with recommended assignments, and (4) migration notes from RBAC v1/coarse roles including dual-read behavior and troubleshooting for permission denied.

Motivation / context
Enterprise customers (Dedicated + Private) are asking for least-privilege guidance and auditability artifacts. Support/Solutions also need a stable reference for: “what does this permission do?”, “which role should I assign?”, and “why am I denied?”. The docs in this PR align to the RBAC v2 enforcement + audit decision logging work landing in platform services.

What’s included
- New docs page: /security/rbac-v2/overview
  - Definitions: Permission, Role, Role Binding, Subject (user/service principal), Scope (org/project/environment/cluster)
  - Enforcement model: deny-by-default, gateway + service checks, consistent 403 semantics
  - Auditability: authz decision context fields (subject/action/resource scope/policy version/decision/denial reason)
- New docs page: /security/rbac-v2/permissions
  - Permission string format + namespacing (redwood.<domain>.<resource>.<action>)
  - Stability + deprecation policy (no silent semantic changes; documented replacements)
  - “Sensitive operations” callouts (key rotation, secret operations, role changes, routing policy edits, audit export)
- New docs page: /security/rbac-v2/role-templates
  - Built-in templates (Org Admin, Security Admin, Billing Admin, Read-only Auditor, Deployment Operator)
  - Guidance for separation of duties (Security Admin ≠ Billing Admin by default)
  - Scope examples for Dedicated/Private (cluster-scoped operator; environment-scoped auditor)
- New docs page: /security/rbac-v2/migration
  - Dual-read/feature flag rollout explanation
  - Expected behavior during transition (v1 roles mapped to v2 templates; denies surfaced with reason codes)
  - Troubleshooting: interpreting denial reason codes; when to escalate; how to request temporary elevation

Non-goals
- This does not document a customer-exposed policy language/ABAC.
- This does not publish internal-only permissions or break-glass runbooks in full; we link to a high-level “Emergency access” section and keep operational steps internal.

Notable details / decisions
- Permission catalog is presented as a curated reference with examples + categories rather than a full dump of every internal action. The page includes a “How to find the exact permission” section pointing to Console Permission Viewer (coming soon) and API error details when debug headers are enabled for admins.
- Migration page explicitly calls out that some previously implicit capabilities may require explicit grants under v2 (deny-by-default).

Screenshots
- N/A (text-only docs). I verified local rendering and navigation.

Checklist
- [x] Content reviewed with Security Eng + PM
- [x] Link checker passes (no broken internal anchors)
- [x] Adds redirect stubs for old RBAC v1 doc URLs
- [x] Notes on audit log fields match current schema naming

Follow-ups
- Add a generated “permissions table” section once the permission registry stabilizes (to avoid churn). Proposed approach: nightly job to export a versioned markdown artifact from the registry used by services.
- Add Console screenshots for role editor and permission viewer once DES-914 lands in product UI.
commits: docs(security): add RBAC v2 overview + navigation entries
docs(security): add permission catalog reference + naming/versioning section
docs(security): add built-in role templates page + scope examples
docs(security): add migration + troubleshooting notes (denial reason codes)
chore(docs): add redirects + fix linkcheck failures
source.review_thread:
Naomi Feldman (PM) — Requested changes: The migration page should be explicit that v2 is deny-by-default and can surface new 403s if customers relied on implicit org-admin powers from legacy roles. Also add a short section on how Support should ask for the denial reason code and request ID.

Elliot Price — Updated migration doc with a “What changes under deny-by-default” section and added a Troubleshooting checklist (request id + denial reason code + scope). Also clarified that legacy role mappings are transitional.

Marcus Lin (Security Eng) — Requested changes: Please avoid publishing internal break-glass procedure details. High-level guidance is fine, but the approval workflow and alerting channels should remain internal.

Elliot Price — Done. Replaced detailed steps with a short “Emergency access” overview and a note that Redwood Support/SecOps coordinate break-glass on request for enterprise contracts.

Kaitlyn Nguyen (Platform) — Comment: Permission format example should match the registry naming (domain/resource/action) and avoid implying version suffixes in the string. Deprecation guidance should reference documented replacements.

Elliot Price — Adjusted examples and added a note that versioning is handled via compatibility guarantees + documented deprecation, not embedded in the permission string.

Tessa Morgan (UX) — Comment: In the troubleshooting section, recommend user-facing copy that doesn’t reveal sensitive authorization details by default, but points admins to debug headers / Console Permission Viewer.

Elliot Price — Updated troubleshooting copy to emphasize least information by default; admin debug path called out separately.

Jordan Lee (Policy) — Approval: Content aligns with current evaluation model and denial reason code strategy.

Final review — Naomi Feldman approved after rewording the “role templates” section to emphasize separation of duties and recommended defaults for regulated orgs.
source.release_notes: Docs: Published RBAC v2 overview, permission reference, role templates, and migration guidance for enterprise customers (Hosted/Dedicated/Private).

source.title: Introduce RAG authoring UX walkthrough and compact step guides
source.author: Mariana Soto
source.description:
Motivation: teams building retrieval-augmented workflows repeatedly ask for concise authoring guidance and a lightweight in-doc walkthrough they can fork into their apps. This PR adds a compact UX-centered walkthrough for RAG authoring, short step guides targeted at SDK users, and a small interactive tutorial embedded in the docs site. The goal is to reduce onboarding time for engineers integrating retrieval + tool-calling flows and to provide reproducible local dev steps and CI smoke checks.

What this PR contains: - a new docs section `docs/rag/ux-walkthrough.md` with a step-by-step narrative of common authoring flows (indexing, prompt scaffolding, tool binding, and retry strategies); - `docs/rag/compact-steps.md`: a 1-page quick checklist for productionizing a RAG endpoint with samples for Python/TS; - `docs/snippets/ux-templates/` tiny prompt templates plus a short JSON schema for tool contracts; - a short interactive tutorial harness integrated into the docs site (static/ux-walkthrough/index.html + a tiny client shim) that can be toggled by readers; - small CI smoke tests (ci/docs-smoke.yaml) which validate build + tutorial boot; - site navigation changes and search tags.

Checklist: - [x] Add walkthrough markdown and compact guides - [x] Add small interactive static example and client shim - [x] Add CI smoke job to ensure tutorial loads in site build - [x] Add minimal screenshots and alt text for accessibility - [x] Request reviews from Docs and SDK owners

Compatibility and scope: This is documentation and static site work only; no runtime or API surface changes. The interactive tutorial uses the existing local mock harness (already in repo) and is gated behind the docs build so it does not affect production infra.

How to review: Read the walkthrough and compact steps for clarity and completeness. Run the site locally via `make site` then navigate to /rag/ux-walkthrough and toggle the embedded tutorial. CI smoke is lightweight and should be run in the docs pipeline.

Related work: references ENG-4127 for onboarding telemetry improvements and PM-923 for the docs initiative. Linked support ticket SUP-341 contains customer requests that motivated the UX examples.

Notes: kept the prose compact on purpose — the walkthrough emphasizes actionable checklists and copy-paste snippets. Screenshots are optimized and intentionally low-res to keep site bundle size small.
commits: 7a2b1c2 - add initial ux-walkthrough draft and compact steps (Mariana Soto)
9f8d3e4 - include interactive static tutorial assets and shim client, add docs smoke CI (Mariana Soto)
c0ffee1 - fix markdown lint issues and alt text, update navigation (Mariana Soto)
b3e2a4d - address review feedback: clarify tool contract JSON schema, reduce screenshots (Mariana Soto)
review_comments: Ethan Park: Nice focused guide. Please add a short note about expected token costs for the sample prompt — otherwise LGTM.
Mariana Soto: Added a small "cost expectations" callout with sample token counts and a note on caching; moved it to compact-steps for visibility.
Lena Fischer: Can we ensure the tool-contract JSON schema stays intentionally minimal? I requested a version that only requires name/type/inputs and points to external docs for advanced fields.
Mariana Soto: Simplified the schema per request and added a link to /docs/tool-contracts for advanced options.
Omar Rahman: CI smoke failed first run due to missing node deps in the docs container, re-ran and it passed. Please add the CI badge to the docs overview page.
Mariana Soto: Added the CI badge to docs/index.md and included a short CI troubleshooting note in the walkthrough.
Lena Fischer: Accessibility nit — image needs better alt text. Fixed in latest commit.
Ethan Park: Approving after minor nits fixed.
ci_checks: site-build: pass - static site built in 86s
accessibility-check: pass - images have alt text
docs-smoke (first run): fail - missing node deps
docs-smoke (rerun): pass - tutorial loaded in headless browser
source.release_notes: Add concise RAG authoring UX walkthrough and compact quickstep guides; include a small interactive tutorial in docs; add docs CI smoke tests; add prompt templates and minimal tool-contract schema.
source.notes_for_ops: Docs-only change. CI smoke job `docs-smoke` was added; if it begins flaking, increase headless browser timeout. No infra rollout needed.
